behaviors.description: The keyword-frequency analyzer assigns the correct sentiment when the review contains only words from the configured positive and negative word lists.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the analyzer’s local scoring/tally/treshold logic. No real OS/network/subprocess is required, and no AI/ML output quality is involved. Cross-module wiring is not necessary to expose this specific failure.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test the keyword analyzer function/object directly with injected positive/negative lists and thresholds; verify sentiment output for representative all-positive, all-negative, and balanced inputs.

behaviors.description: The explanation analyzer produces reasoning that references specific phrases from the original review, not generic boilerplate.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on content quality of generated natural-language reasoning from a real model/analyzer. A stubbed response can always be made to pass and would hide real failures in phrase grounding.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real explanation analyzer on a review set and assert outputs include concrete phrase-level references traceable to each input (not generic templates), with repeated runs/cases to detect model variability.

behaviors.description: When a review contains mixed signals (both positive and negative keywords), the sentiment score reflects the configured tie-breaking rule.
behaviors.justification: Failure is in deterministic tie-break logic inside the keyword analyzer. No real infrastructure or model output is needed; module wiring is not required to reproduce the bug.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test mixed-keyword inputs at tie boundaries with different configured tie-break rules; verify exact sentiment chosen in each rule mode.

behaviors.description: The explanation for a sarcastic review (e.g., "Great, another broken feature") correctly identifies the negative intent despite positive surface words.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on nuanced semantic/pragmatic interpretation (sarcasm) by the real explanation model. Deterministic mocks cannot validate this judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real explanation analyzer on a sarcasm-focused dataset and assert explanations indicate negative intent with evidence from the text, tracking consistency across multiple runs.

behaviors.description: Both outputs (score and explanation) are returned together in a single response, with the explanation referencing the same sentiment direction as the score.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module orchestration plus polarity consistency, but one side is model-generated text. Integration-only stubs can verify packaging, not whether real model output stays directionally aligned with computed score. Not Workflow: only one agentic decision point is required here, not multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Run the full service path with real keyword scoring and real explanation generation; assert single response contains both fields and explanation polarity matches returned score across varied inputs.
